conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let me start by asking: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. His name is Alex, he's 14 years old. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me gather some details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

First, I need to understand your relationship with Alex: **Are you Alex's parent, or do you have parental responsibility for him?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. Now I need to understand Alex's living situation: **Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Let me ask a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Are you or your partner responsible for paying for things like Alex's food, clothing, and other living costs?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I pay for all of Alex's needs - his food, clothing, and everything else he requires.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. A couple more questions:

**Are you currently living or working in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't currently live in the UK. I'm living abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. This is an important factor for Child Benefit eligibility.

Let me read the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information about eligibility when living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. Based on the official guidance, I can see that living abroad is a crucial factor. The rules state that "You normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK."

However, there are some exceptions. Let me ask: **Are you living in certain specific countries (such as within the European Economic Area), or are you a Crown servant (working for the UK government abroad)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant and I'm not living in one of those specific countries that would qualify. I'm just living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me now assess your eligibility based on the official Child Benefit rules.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Assessment Result

I'm sorry to inform you that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

While you meet most of the requirements - Alex is 14 years old (under 16), you are his parent, he lives with you, and you're financially responsible for all his needs - there is one crucial requirement you don't meet: **the UK residence requirement**.

According to the official UK government guidance, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 **and you live in the UK**. There are limited exceptions to this rule, such as:
- Living in certain specific countries (like some within the European Economic Area)
- Being a Crown servant (working for the UK government abroad)

Since you've confirmed that you're living abroad and don't fall into either of these exception categories, unfortunately you cannot claim Child Benefit at this time.

If your circumstances change and you return to live in the UK in the future, you may then be able to claim Child Benefit for Alex (as long as he still meets the age and other eligibility criteria at that time).

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 14 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 14 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 14 years old, which meets the age requirement (under 16). You are Alex's parent and he lives with you, and you are responsible for all his needs including food, clothing, and other living costs. However, according to the official UK government guidance, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 AND you live in the UK. The guidance states there are exceptions for those living in certain countries or Crown servants working abroad, but you have confirmed you do not fall into either of these exception categories. Since you are living abroad and do not meet any of the specific exceptions, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets most of the basic requirements for Child Benefit - they are the parent of a 14-year-old child who lives with them and for whom they are financially responsible. However, the residence requirement is not met. The claimant is living abroad and does not qualify under any of the exceptions (such as being a Crown servant or living in specific qualifying countries). Therefore, they are not eligible for Child Benefit.

Since you are living abroad and do not meet any of the specific exceptions, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
